meta.description: Module 01 — RSS finder, diagnostic, Mpemba scenario, LD scan
meta.provenance: FULLY COMPUTED — QuTiP mesolve. Not hand-authored. Pre-registration pending.

rss_check.description: Spin in RSS ⊗ motion thermal. Non-monotonic: D rises then returns. Confirms RSS is global not local fixed point.

mpemba_scenario.description: State A (near north pole, bz≈+1) vs State B (interior, bz≈-0.35, bx≈0.19). A starts farther (D0=0.545) and B starts closer (D0=0.162). A crosses below B at t*≈7.4, re-crosses at t*≈8.7. Transient crossing driven by non-monotonic spin-motion entanglement dynamics. States with purely longitudinal deviation show NO crossing — transverse components required.
mpemba_scenario.physics_note: The crossing is non-trivial: it requires initial states with transverse Bloch components. Purely longitudinal pairs (same bz axis) show no crossing in the Markovian limit. The non-monotonic dynamics of state A reflects spin-motion entanglement building then releasing — the same mechanism shown in the RSS diagnostic. The non-Markovian bath (Strachan et al.) is expected to produce more pronounced and persistent crossings.

ld_scan.description: Lamb-Dicke correction scan. Same state pair (A, B) above. t_end=60.
